topic: Two colleagues are both considered for the same promotion
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed with an aggressive, high-pitched whine, mirroring the frantic rhythm in Sarah’s chest. She kept smoothing the lapel of her blazer, a nervous habit she couldn't shake. Every time Mark walked past her desk, exchanging a brief, breezy nod with the department head, a cold knot tightened beneath her ribs. *He always gets the nod,* she thought, staring intently at the spreadsheet in front of her, the numbers blurring into meaningless streaks. She took a deep, shallow breath, the air tasting thin. When Mark finally approached, his smile was wide and easy, and the casual way he leaned in to discuss the Q3 projections felt like a physical weight pressing down on her shoulders. "Great work on that presentation, Sarah," he said, his tone perfectly modulated, leaving her tongue suddenly dry. She managed a tight, "Thanks, Mark," forcing a smile that felt brittle around the edges.

[story 2]

Liam paced the length of his small cubicle, his movements jerky and repetitive. The clock on the wall seemed to tick with malicious intent, each second an accusation. He kept running a hand through his already disheveled hair, feeling the heat rising in his neck. He re-read the job description for the senior role for the tenth time, the bolded requirements seeming to mock his inadequacy. Every time he saw Chloe confidently presenting her case to the committee, a wave of intense pressure washed over him, making his palms sweat. He gripped his lukewarm coffee mug so tightly his knuckles turned white. "I just need to be more visible," he muttered under his breath, the words sounding hollow even to his own ears. He glanced over at Chloe, whose relaxed posture seemed to radiate an effortless competence that made Liam feel acutely small and inadequate.

[story 3]

Maria meticulously organized her desk, stacking files with an almost obsessive precision. The silence in her workspace was deafening, amplifying the rapid drumming of her heart against her ribs. She kept catching her reflection in the dark monitor screen, noticing the faint tremor in her hands. The thought of the promotion—the one she had been aiming for since January—felt like a massive, insurmountable mountain. When David stopped by to ask for clarification on a report, Maria’s voice came out higher pitched than intended. "Yes, I've already factored in the regional variances," she managed, the words tumbling out in a rush. She avoided his gaze, focusing instead on the dust motes dancing in the afternoon sunbeam slicing through the blinds, wishing she could simply disappear into the beige walls.
